name: Depopulated area of Castarlenas from Capella
region_intro: Hiking around Capella offers a diverse landscape characterized by the Isábena river valley, historical sites, and tracks connecting to a reservoir. The region features rolling hills, such as the one hosting the Basilica of the Virgen de la Peña, providing scenic views. This area is ideal for those seeking easy hikes near Capella with historical interest and natural beauty. Best hiking trails around Capella The most popular hiking route is Basílica de Nuestra Señora de la Peña y Ermita de San Pedro - circular desde Graus, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 25 minutes to complete. This circular path offers views of the historic basilica and surrounding hills. Another top favourite among local hikers is Grustán and the Hermitages of Graus Loop, a moderate 5.4 miles (8.7 km) path. This route explores the local terrain, passing by several hermitages. Local hikers also love the Depopulated area of Castarlenas from Capella, a 9.2 miles (14.8 km) trail leading through varied rural landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 54 minutes. Hiking around Capella is defined by river valleys, historical structures like the Romanesque bridge, and tracks leading to reservoirs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy circular walks to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Capella are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Capella's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Capella?
A: Capella offers a diverse selection of over 50 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 21 easy trails and 29 moderate options, ensuring there's something for everyone to explore.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Capella?
A: The hiking trails in Capella are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 50 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the region's blend of natural beauty, historical sites like the Romanesque bridge, and the scenic views, particularly from elevated points such as the hill hosting the Basilica of the Virgen de la Peña.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Capella?
A: Yes, Capella has several easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed walk. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938969473/basilica-de-nuestra-senora-de-la-pena-y-ermita-de-san-pedro-circular-desde-graus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basílica de Nuestra Señora de la Peña y Ermita de San Pedro - circular desde Graus&lt;/a&gt;, a 2.7-mile (4.4 km) circular path offering historical views. Another easy route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10466449" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Abajo – Puente de Abajo loop from Graus&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.2 miles (5.2 km) long.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Capella?
A: Yes, Capella features several excellent circular hiking routes. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938969473/basilica-de-nuestra-senora-de-la-pena-y-ermita-de-san-pedro-circular-desde-graus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basílica de Nuestra Señora de la Peña y Ermita de San Pedro - circular desde Graus&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views and historical interest. Another popular loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938983315/grustan-and-the-hermitages-of-graus-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grustán and the Hermitages of Graus Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.4-mile (8.7 km) path exploring local terrain and hermitages.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see while hiking around Capella?
A: The Capella region is rich in history and offers several landmarks along its trails. You can visit the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5088743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Capella Romanesque Bridge&lt;/a&gt; over the Isábena river, or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4443331" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basilica of the Virgen de la Peña&lt;/a&gt;, perched on a hill with fantastic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2905086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abandoned Village of Castarlenas&lt;/a&gt; is also a unique historical site to discover, accessible via routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e943050348/depopulated-area-of-castarlenas-from-capella" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Depopulated area of Castarlenas from Capella&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Capella?
A: Many of Capella's easy trails are suitable for families. The region's gentle terrain, historical points of interest, and tracks connecting to the reservoir provide engaging environments for all ages. Consider shorter, less strenuous routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938969473/basilica-de-nuestra-senora-de-la-pena-y-ermita-de-san-pedro-circular-desde-graus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basílica de Nuestra Señora de la Peña y Ermita de San Pedro - circular desde Graus&lt;/a&gt; for a pleasant family outing.

Q: Are hiking trails in Capella dog-friendly?
A: Generally, the rural hiking trails around Capella are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially near livestock or in protected areas, and to carry waste bags to maintain the cleanliness of the trails.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Capella?
A: The Capella region, located in Spain, typically offers pleasant hiking conditions during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) when temperatures are milder. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are advisable, while winter offers a different, often quieter, experience, though some higher elevation paths might be cooler.

Q: Are there any hikes near Capella that include options for a pub lunch?
A: While specific 'pub lunch' stops directly on trails are not explicitly highlighted, the Capella region is close to towns like Graus and La Puebla de Castro. Many trails, especially those starting or ending near these settlements, will allow you to easily access local eateries and restaurants for a meal after your hike. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10466451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor de Graus – Ermita de San Miguel loop from Graus&lt;/a&gt; starts and ends in Graus, providing convenient access to local amenities.

Q: Are there any wild swimming spots near Capella hikes?
A: The Capella region features tracks that connect to a reservoir, which could offer opportunities for refreshing dips. While specific designated wild swimming spots are not detailed, exploring the areas around the Isábena river and the reservoir might reveal suitable locations for a cool-down after your hike. Always exercise caution and check local regulations before swimming in natural waters.

Q: How long do hikes in Capella typically take?
A: Hike durations in Capella vary significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. Easy trails, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938969473/basilica-de-nuestra-senora-de-la-pena-y-ermita-de-san-pedro-circular-desde-graus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basílica de Nuestra Señora de la Peña y Ermita de San Pedro - circular desde Graus&lt;/a&gt;, can be completed in about 1 hour 25 minutes. Moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e943050348/depopulated-area-of-castarlenas-from-capella" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Depopulated area of Castarlenas from Capella&lt;/a&gt;, might take around 4 hours 54 minutes for its 9.2 miles (14.8 km) length.
tips: Romanesque bridge over the river Isábena with eight eyes and vaults that range from very low segmental arches at the beginnings to the three-centered vault of the main span.

Castarlenas, an abandoned village in a beautiful landscape. Forest as far as the eye can see. In the distance you can see the Barasona reservoir, three or four villages, the highest mountains in the Pyrenees, and in between pure nature.
